image_prompts.prompt: Hook visual: Here's what science actually says about reconsolidation memory.
Context visual: When we recall a memory, it can change. This is known as reconsolidation.
Escalation visual: Every time we remember, our brain rewrites the memory, making us vulnerable to errors.
Evidence visual: Research shows reconsolidation can alter emotions associated with memories. Feeling different about your past?
Payoff visual: This means our memories aren’t static. They evolve, shaping our identity in unexpected ways.
Cta visual: Want to dive deeper into how your memories shape you? Hit subscribe!
